summary: Closed, unmerged MP matching slice touching src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c and src/melee/mp/mplib.c. The diff moved a Vec3/PAD_STACK declaration in mpColl_80046224_LeftWall and merged several mpLib spawn vertex ID arrays into one larger array with pointer-offset #define aliases. A maintainer closed the PR as incorrect, specifically calling out wrong field-size implications based on offsets and the define usage as a regression. The decomp-dev report for this PR reported no changes.

key_files.role: Moved local Vec3 nrm and PAD_STACK(0x44) declaration outward in mpColl_80046224_LeftWall.
Collapsed separate spawn vertex ID arrays into a larger mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds array and recreated removed arrays with pointer-offset #define aliases.

what_changed.text: In mpColl_80046224_LeftWall, Vec3 nrm and PAD_STACK(0x44) were moved from inside the nested left-wall branch to the outer block after Vec3 vec.

In mplib.c, mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds changed from length 0x15 to 0xAE and absorbed the entries previously held by mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds, and mpLib_ExitVtxIds, with zero padding inserted between groups.
The removed spawn vertex ID arrays were replaced by preprocessor aliases: mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds as mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds + 0x16, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds as +0x66, and mpLib_ExitVtxIds as +0x80.

The PR body described the work as a split from #2617 scoped to src/melee/mp, with preliminary unsplit-branch matching progress and local diff-check evidence; the PR-specific decomp-dev report later stated 'No changes'.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body Preliminary Report and Local Checks

what_changed.text: The PR was closed after maintainer feedback that the changes looked incorrect and that define usage was a regression.

smart_moves.text: The author split a broader branch by directory/subsystem and documented the exact scope and changed files, which made the MP slice easier to review.
The PR body distinguished preliminary full-branch scores from authoritative per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes.'

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not treat adjacent-looking data tables as one larger C array with pointer-offset aliases unless section/symbol/offset evidence supports the ownership and element sizing; in this PR, reviewer feedback said the define-based layout was incorrect and a regression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds size 0x15 -> 0xAE and alias defines

decomp_lessons.lesson: For PR-local validation claims, prefer the per-PR decomp-dev report over broad unsplit-branch progress claims; here the PR body gave unsplit progress, while the bot report for the slice said 'No changes'.

naming_conventions.convention: Keep separate data symbol names such as mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds, and mpLib_ExitVtxIds as real declarations unless evidence supports replacing them; do not preserve those names only as pointer-offset #define aliases.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed static s16 declarations and added #define aliases
issue_comment: PsiLupan: 'usage of the define is a regression'

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and local lifetime/declaration movement were used in mpColl_80046224_LeftWall by moving Vec3 nrm and PAD_STACK(0x44) outward, but this PR provides no objdiff evidence that the tactic improved a match.

The mplib.c hunk attempted data-layout matching by consolidating several s16 arrays and recreating former array symbols as pointer offsets into the first array; reviewer feedback rejected this tactic.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: Overall, these changes look incorrect. The defines even show that the size changes of the particular fields being referenced would be wrong based on offset. Likewise, the usage of the define is a regression, so I'm closing this PR.

follow_up_queries: PR 2627 PsiLupan usage of the define is a regression

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text: PR body and maintainer issue comment
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for both changed files
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report and closing comment
notes: There were no review comments and no formal review records; reviewer feedback came from an issue comment.
The PR body's matching percentages are explicitly from the full unsplit source branch, not necessarily this MP slice.
The PR-specific decomp-dev bot report says 'No changes', so no function match or score improvement should be inferred for this PR.
The maintainer comment supports that the define-based data layout was rejected, but does not provide a detailed corrected layout.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2627 rejected pointer-offset #define aliases for MP spawn vertex tables
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2627, mplib.c replaced separate spawn vertex ID arrays with one enlarged mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds array plus #define aliases at offsets. Maintainer feedback called the changes incorrect and the define usage a regression, citing offset/field-size problems.
scope: static s16 spawn vertex ID tables

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify that data-table aliases via #define need ownership and offset evidence
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or strengthening guidance that adjacent data tables should not be merged into one oversized C array and re-exposed through pointer-offset #define aliases unless section ownership, symbol metadata, element size, and offset evidence prove that layout. PR #2627 is an example where a maintainer rejected this as incorrect and a define regression.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already discourage define aliases and fake data storage, but this PR provides a concrete rejected pattern involving coalesced arrays and pointer-offset aliases.

rejection_notes: Do not record the preliminary unsplit-branch matching percentages as achieved by PR #2627; the PR body says per-PR reporting is authoritative and the bot report says 'No changes'.
Do not infer that the PAD_STACK movement in mpcoll.c was accepted or beneficial; the PR was closed and no objdiff evidence is present.
Do not infer the exact correct sizes or ownership of the mpLib spawn vertex arrays beyond the reviewer statement that the proposed define/offset layout was wrong.
